summary: Iteration 35 — tested background graph-extraction daemon + 'Retry with deeper graph' narrow-mode button. All 10 backend pytest cases pass. Frontend sidebar renders the Knowledge Graph section with fuchsia progress bar + running phase badge; narrow answer exposes retry-deeper button which correctly appends a new multihop assistant message with Graph·Multi-hop badge. Daemon is actively progressing (extracted grew 62→66 in ~80s of testing).
critical_code_review_comments: daemons/graph_extraction.py uses `asyncio.run()` per cycle inside a blocking thread — works but creates a new event loop each cycle. Acceptable for a long-running daemon with 20s cadence; keep in mind if cycle frequency increases.

compliance.py:287 POST /graph/start-extraction always spawns a new thread — the docstring calls this idempotent, which is true for DB (upserts) but will leak threads on repeated calls (each thread blocks on the cycle loop forever). Low risk since UI button only shows when phase='not_started', but consider a module-level singleton guard.
compliance.py:322 /graph/query opens a pymongo client and closes it only on success; if build_subgraph throws the client is leaked. Wrap in try/finally (same pattern already used in smart-research).

ComplianceResearchPanel.js:700 retry-deeper calls `ask(messages[i - 1]?.content || m.question, 'multihop')` — if the user deleted messages, i-1 may not be the user prompt. Safe fallback to m.question is in place so OK, minor.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Daemon was already running at phase=running, cycle_count=2 when tests started. After ~80s of tests it was at cycle_count≈6, extracted=66. /graph/start-extraction spawns a new thread every call — idempotent vs DB but thread-count grows over time. If future tests need a fresh start, restart backend supervisor. Smart-research force_mode='multihop' takes ~25-40s so pytest timeouts must be >60s.
